overview.brief_summary: A basic attack scenario targeting communications networks using drive-by downloads and exploitation of public-facing applications.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2015-0313 (Suggested): Use-after-free vulnerability in Adobe Flash Player allows remote attackers to execute code.
stage.description: The attacker uses a drive-by download to exploit an unpatched vulnerability in SD-WAN devices, allowing them to compromise the device via a delivery method.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2018-2628 (Suggested): Oracle WebLogic Server contains an unspecified vulnerability which can allow an unauthenticated attacker with T3 network access to compromise the server.
stage.description: The attacker exploits an unpatched vulnerability in a public-facing application to gain access to the network via a delivery method.

stage.name: Execution
stage.technique_name: T1094: Custom Command and Control Protocol
stage.kev: CVE-2022-3723 (Suggested): Google Chromium V8 Engine contains a type confusion vulnerability that allows a remote attacker to potentially exploit heap corruption via a crafted HTML page.
stage.description: The attacker establishes a custom command and control channel to maintain persistence on the compromised network.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-24: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: The attacker exfiltrates sensitive data from the compromised network, potentially including communications-related information.

Scenario.summary: This attack scenario involves a basic attack chain targeting communications networks using drive-by downloads and exploitation of public-facing applications. The attacker gains initial access through unpatched vulnerabilities in SD-WAN devices and public-facing applications, then establishes a custom command and control protocol to maintain persistence. Finally, they exfiltrate sensitive data from the compromised network.
